title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Cardinality Forensics
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an audited service-metrics query pack and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Aggregate Functions
steps.instruction:
debug case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 5 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

Inspect the Aggregate Functions trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Metric Validation
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-metric-validation-19rlx8" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; aggregate grain, denominator, or reconciliation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Metric Validation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Metric Validation into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Metric Validation, keep Metric Validation connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-metric-validation-19rlx8
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT COUNT(*) AS all_tickets, COUNT(owner_id) AS owned_tickets
FROM tickets;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Metric Validation
steps.instruction:

Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 3 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile totals, denominators, missing groups, and changed cases before publishing an aggregate claim.
